title: podman container rm: remove pod
body:
Support removing the entire pod when --depend is used on an infra
container.  --all now implies --depend to properly support removing all
containers and not error out when hitting infra containers.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Can we only do this when `--depends` from Dan's recent PR is set? We can have `--all` also imply `--depends`. Basically, a `podman rm -f $INFRA` should not remove the pod on its own, but a `podman rm -f --depends $INFRA` should, because the extra CLI arg implies you are OK with Podman taking down extra containers.

> Can we only do this when `--depends` from Dan's recent PR is set? We can have `--all` also imply `--depends`. Basically, a `podman rm -f $INFRA` should not remove the pod on its own, but a `podman rm -f --depends $INFRA` should, because the extra CLI arg implies you are OK with Podman taking down extra containers.

I am OK with that, sure. I mostly care about `--all --force` to really nuke everything.
@mheon: should it require both `--force` and `--depends` or only `--depends`? I think that only `--depends` should suffice for attempting to remove and `--force` in case it's running?
Yep, that SGTM. `--depends` is enough to recursively remove, `--force` is only required if they're running.

Reproducer:
```console
$ printf "FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610\nRUN touch /foo\nRUN sleep 30\n" | bin/podman-remote build -t foo -
STEP 1/3: FROM quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610
STEP 2/3: RUN touch /foo
--> 714e34dc000
STEP 3/3: RUN sleep 30
^C         <------ make sure to ^C in the middle of the sleep
$ bin/podman-remote image prune -f --external
<sha>
$ bin/podman ps --external
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                              COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
47a674f877d4  quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610  buildah     3 minutes ago  storage                 testimage-working-container
$ bin/podman-remote rm 47a6
Error: no container with name or ID "47a6" found: no such container 
$ echo $?
1
```
This is the bug. The expected error is "container xxx is mounted and cannot be removed without using force", and the expected exit status is 2, not 1.
Thanks, Ed! I already have a local fix for it; needed some more massaging of the remote code.
